text.title: AI Failure Mode Taxonomy Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules every application of AI Failure Mode Taxonomy enforces. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every observed instance gets exactly one primary label from the AI Failure Mode Taxonomy taxonomy. Secondary tags are allowed and tracked separately.
rule.rationale: Multi-primary labels destroy aggregation. Dashboards over-count, postmortems get muddled.

rule.statement: A label is added to the taxonomy only after ≥3 instances justify it AND the owner approves. No ad-hoc labels.
rule.rationale: Unconstrained label creation = no labels. Every team-member spins their own variant.

rule.statement: Re-classifications preserve history: the prior label and timestamp stay on record. Label rewrites are append-only, not destructive.
rule.rationale: Destructive re-tagging makes trend lines meaningless. Append-only preserves the learning that drove the re-label.

rule.statement: Each label maps to a defined remediation lane. A label that does not change action is decoration, not classification.
rule.rationale: Labels exist to route response. If 'hallucination' and 'tool-misuse' lead to the same fix, merge them.

example.title: Routed failure category
example.code: Label: tool-misuse Definition: agent called a tool with arguments outside the tool's declared schema Detector: schema-validation error in logs Remediation: re-train tool description / add schema enforcement layer
example.note: Label maps cleanly to a remediation lane. Aggregation reveals where to invest.

example.title: Catch-all category
example.code: Label: 'AI weirdness' Definition: when the model does something strange Remediation: investigate
example.note: Bucket label that absorbs everything. Dashboards over-count, postmortems are useless.
